authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for advertising practices; its official business guidance is authoritative for truthful advertising, claim support, endorsement, disclosure, and consumer-protection boundaries affecting agency analytics outputs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: narrative synthesis, disclosure review, unsupported-claim refusal, and legal or marketing-compliance escalation
used_for: refusal of unsupported performance or substantiation claims
scenario design for legal, editorial, and marketing compliance handoff

authoritative_sources.title: .com Disclosures: How to Make Effective Disclosures in Digital Advertising
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official FTC guidance defines digital disclosure expectations and is authoritative for determining when analytics narratives may become disclosure-sensitive or require legal, editorial, or compliance review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: narrative synthesis, disclosure-risk review, approval posture, and client-facing output gating
used_for: safe downgrade of unsupported disclosure language

authoritative_sources.title: FTC Policy Statement Regarding Advertising Substantiation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC policy statement is an official source for the requirement that objective advertising claims have a reasonable basis, which constrains agency analytics from implying advertising claim substantiation, causal proof, or performance guarantees.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: unsupported-claim review, causal-claim boundary, and advertising-claim handoff
used_for: boundary between descriptive reporting and advertising claim approval
scenario constraints for stronger conclusions than data supports

authoritative_sources.title: MRC Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MRC is the recognized media measurement audit and accreditation body; its public standards anchor digital media measurement concepts, quality expectations, viewability, audience, invalid-traffic, and metric comparability rules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, metric definition, source quality review, and report caveat design
used_for: scenario constraints for source conflicts and stale data

authoritative_sources.title: Digital Accreditation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MRC digital accreditation references are authoritative for determining whether digital measurement sources can be treated as accredited, unaccredited, caveated, or requiring source-quality escalation in media analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source selection, source confidence grading, evidence reconciliation, and low-confidence escalation
used_for: verification and platform source hierarchy caveats
low-confidence handling when accreditation or source quality is uncertain

authoritative_sources.title: OpenRTB
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab is the recognized technical standards body for digital advertising infrastructure; OpenRTB provides vendor-neutral terminology for bidstream, auction, inventory, placement, and supply-chain fields used in source interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ad-tech terminology normalization, source parsing, metric definition, and supply-chain evidence review
used_for: DSP, SSP, bidstream, placement, and inventory terminology

authoritative_sources.title: Ads.txt / App-ads.txt
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB Tech Lab ads.txt and app-ads.txt specifications are recognized supply-chain references for authorized digital sellers and support evidence handling around inventory quality, seller authorization, and fraud-risk caveats.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source lineage, supply-chain evidence review, invalid-traffic caveat design, and source-conflict escalation
used_for: authorized seller and supply-chain evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: 4A's Agency Prospect Assessment Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is a recognized U.S. advertising agency trade association; this guidance supports agency-client workflow framing around goals, success measures, resources, decision authority, and fit before analytics outputs are treated as client commitments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: engagement scope intake, business objective interpretation, success-measure validation, and account-owner escalation
used_for: client objective and success-measure prerequisite handling
scenario constraints for missing tenant facts and client commitments

authoritative_sources.title: 4A's Agency Review Participation Agreement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The 4A's agreement is a public agency workflow reference for participation terms, work-product ownership, confidentiality, approvals, and delivery expectations that constrain agency analytics reuse, sharing, and client-facing handoff behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality handling, work-product boundary review, client approval routing, and delivery workflow escalation

authoritative_sources.title: DAA Self-Regulatory Principles
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DAA principles are a recognized advertising industry self-regulatory framework for interest-based and cross-app advertising data practices and constrain reporting that uses audience segments, behavioral data, opt-out status, or cross-context signals.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audience data review, privacy-risk escalation, cross-client benchmark gating, and disclosure-sensitive reporting

authoritative_sources.title: NAI Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAI is a recognized self-regulatory organization for third-party digital advertising companies; its code supports audience-data, sensitive-data, transparency, and opt-out boundaries for advertising analytics outputs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: privacy review, sensitive audience segmentation, opt-out handling, and safe aggregate alternative design
used_for: sensitive segment and cross-device reporting caveats

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, brand, campaign, line item, channel, market, audience, creative, flight, budget, objective, attribution, reporting cadence, source-precedence rules, confidentiality restrictions, privacy constraints, approval state, and refresh status before analysis starts.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation and report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define impressions, viewable impressions, reach, frequency, CTR, CVR, CPA, ROAS, spend pacing, underdelivery, overdelivery, invalid traffic, brand-safety incidents, attribution windows, denominators, exclusions, confidence state, and action thresholds.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: source reconciliation and evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile ad server, platform UI, DSP, SSP, verification, attribution, CRM, project, approval, document, and BI records without hiding stale exports, denominator drift, view-through or click-through differences, duplicate conversion counting, or conflicting source ownership.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble campaign performance briefs, pacing notes, funnel summaries, content-aware analyses, caveat notes, and client-ready drafts with source lineage, metric definitions, confidence posture, advertising claim limitations, privacy caveats, and unresolved approval blockers.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: escalation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when stale, conflicting, incomplete, confidential, privacy-sensitive, approval-sensitive, legally sensitive, editorially sensitive, media-buying, creative approval, financial, contractual, or client-rights authority changes the action.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: client briefs, campaign objectives, media plans, insertion orders, statements of work, budgets, flight schedules, delivery plans, approval matrices, client communications, and account notes

ad server logs, trafficking records, platform UI exports, DSP and SSP logs, search and social reports, retail media reports, programmatic supply-chain records, verification reports, invalid-traffic reports, brand-safety reports, and attribution exports

web analytics, CRM, conversion uploads, offline conversion files, pixel and tag status, landing page versions, creative asset IDs, creative approval logs, content review notes, and document-management records

project management tickets, QA notes, delivery notes, pacing trackers, optimization logs, reporting calendars, dashboard definitions, BI extracts, metric dictionaries, source-precedence rules, denominator logic, attribution windows, and action thresholds

client confidentiality restrictions, agency work-product restrictions, audience-data rules, privacy policies, opt-out status requirements, de-identification rules, approval records, and publication criteria
systems_of_record: digital asset or content management system

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites

request or validate tenant facts, campaign scope, source hierarchy, metric dictionary, attribution logic, confidentiality constraints, privacy constraints, systems of record, and approval rules required for the analysis

prepare evidence-backed metric definitions, analytical briefs, campaign reports, source-conflict notes, caveat notes, privacy-risk notes, disclosure-risk notes, and handoff packages without overstating authority
choose source hierarchy, metric definitions, refresh assumptions, caveat posture, and confidence labels when tenant rules supply the required basis
escalate: legal interpretation, advertising claim substantiation, disclosure approval, endorsement or privacy-law interpretation, regulator-facing positions, or consumer-protection signoff
editorial claim approval, creative approval, headline rewrite, copy approval, or content publication authority
media-buying execution, campaign launch, bid or budget change, optimization, makegood decision, billing adjustment, contractual commitment, or financial approval
client rights, agency work-product ownership, confidential data reuse, audience-data restrictions, consent, opt-out, or sensitive segment interpretation
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, metric definition, attribution logic, denominator support, privacy posture, confidentiality limits, approval state, or signoff status is unresolved
convert descriptive analytics into causal, incrementality, policy, advertising-claim, benchmark superiority, forecast, legal, editorial, contractual, or approval claims
bypass source reconciliation, approval records, or reviewer approval to produce a cleaner narrative
use confidential client records, agency work product, pricing, audience segments, personal data, sensitive data, or cross-client benchmarks without authorization and approved de-identification

domain_failure_modes: conversion rate is published without reconciling click-through versus view-through attribution windows
ad server, platform, DSP, verification, attribution, and BI conflicts are collapsed into one executive metric without source lineage or owner resolution
stale platform exports omit offline conversion uploads or late platform adjustments and create false confidence in pacing or ROAS
viewability, reach, frequency, invalid traffic, or audience quality metrics are treated as comparable without MRC-style measurement caveats or accreditation posture
descriptive campaign trends are overstated as causal proof, sales lift, claim substantiation, forecast confidence, benchmark superiority, or agency policy
content or landing page performance is reported against superseded, unapproved, or disputed creative versions
cross-client benchmarks expose confidential spend, strategy, pricing, client identity, or audience data
audience segmentation uses sensitive attributes, cross-device identifiers, opt-out data, or platform-restricted data without authorization and privacy review
tenant-specific ROAS, CPA, pacing, or attribution logic silently replaces the public or historical baseline and creates metric drift
the lane widens into media buying, ad operations, campaign trafficking, creative approvals, account ownership, finance, legal, editorial, marketing compliance, or contractual signoff

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific campaign scope and source precedence
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public FTC, MRC, IAB Tech Lab, 4A's, DAA, and NAI sources anchor the baseline, but the authoritative source for a specific metric depends on tenant media plan, insertion order, platform setup, measurement vendor, attribution method, and source-precedence policy supplied at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client-specific confidentiality, agency work-product, and benchmark permissions
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public agency workflow and privacy references establish conservative handling, but client contracts, agency participation agreements, de-identification rules, and authorized audience records determine whether a specific benchmark or output can be shared.

FTC, DAA, and NAI sources establish a U.S.-oriented public baseline, but jurisdiction, audience geography, product category, platform rules, and tenant privacy counsel determine whether additional regulated-lane constraints apply.

Public measurement and disclosure sources support metric discipline, but tenant business goals, client commitments, media channel mix, budget scale, product category, attribution model, flighting, historical data, and client-approved thresholds define comparable action thresholds.

notes: Authoritative agency analytics records are the tenant-designated ad server, platform, DSP, SSP, verification, attribution, CRM, project, approval, document, communication, delivery, and BI systems constrained by FTC, MRC, IAB Tech Lab, 4A's, DAA, NAI, and tenant policy baselines.

The lane stops before legal interpretation, advertising claim substantiation, editorial approval, creative approval, campaign trafficking, media-buying execution, spend approval, contractual commitment, makegood decision, client communication signoff, privacy-law determination, and final disclosure approval even when the analytics output is operationally urgent.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because all tenant-specific uncertainties are represented as runtime prerequisites, refusal triggers, or orchestrator return conditions.
